usage_notes: Abbreviated as 'Uni' in informal speech; commonly used for four-year higher education institutions
common_collocations: an der Universität studieren, die Universität besuchen, eine Universität gründen
image_context: A large university campus with students walking between buildings
example_1: Sie studiert Medizin an der Universität Berlin.
example_1_translation: Ela estuda medicina na Universidade de Berlim.
example_2: Nach dem Abitur möchte er an die Universität gehen.
example_2_translation: Após o vestibular, ele quer ir para a universidade.
example_3: Die Universität bietet viele internationale Programme an.
example_3_translation: A universidade oferece muitos programas internacionais.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on fourth syllable: u-ni-ver-si-TÄT; the 'ä' sounds like /ɛ/
memory_hook: Sounds like 'university' in English — almost identical
related_words: die Hochschule, das Studium, der Student, studieren
common_mistakes: Using 'auf der' instead of 'an der Universität'

usage_notes: Used for academic degrees (e.g. 'Bachelorabschluss') as well as the end of something; context-dependent meaning
common_collocations: einen Abschluss machen, den Abschluss erreichen, ohne Abschluss
synonyms: der Abschluss, das Zeugnis, der Grad
image_context: A student holding a graduation diploma at a ceremony
example_1: Sie hat ihren Abschluss an der Uni Frankfurt gemacht.
example_1_translation: Ela fez sua graduação na Universidade de Frankfurt.
example_2: Ohne einen guten Abschluss ist es schwer, Arbeit zu finden.
example_2_translation: Sem uma boa graduação, é difícil encontrar trabalho.
example_3: Er arbeitet hart, um seinen Abschluss rechtzeitig zu bekommen.
example_3_translation: Ele trabalha duro para conseguir seu diploma a tempo.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: AB-schluss; 'sch' = /ʃ/
memory_hook: 'Ab' means off/away, 'Schluss' means end — the 'end' that takes you away from school
related_words: das Diplom, der Bachelor, der Master, abschließen
common_mistakes: Confusing 'der Abschluss' (degree) with 'der Schluss' (conclusion/end)

usage_notes: Used for both academic exams and official inspections (e.g. car inspection); 'Prüfung ablegen' = to sit an exam
common_collocations: eine Prüfung ablegen, die Prüfung bestehen, durch die Prüfung fallen
synonyms: der Test, die Klausur, das Examen
image_context: Students sitting at desks in a classroom writing an exam
example_1: Morgen habe ich eine wichtige Prüfung in Mathematik.
example_1_translation: Amanhã tenho uma prova importante de matemática.
example_2: Er hat die Prüfung mit sehr gut bestanden.
example_2_translation: Ele passou na prova com nota muito boa.
example_3: Sie lernt jeden Tag, um für die Prüfung vorbereitet zu sein.
example_3_translation: Ela estuda todos os dias para estar preparada para a prova.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: PRÜ-fung; 'ü' = /yː/ (rounded front vowel)
memory_hook: 'Prüfen' means to check/test — a Prüfung is the event where you get checked
related_words: prüfen, der Test, die Klausur, bestehen, durchfallen
common_mistakes: Saying 'die Prüfung machen' instead of 'die Prüfung ablegen' (more formal)

usage_notes: Often used in singular without article in academic contexts; 'Forschung betreiben' = to conduct research
common_collocations: Forschung betreiben, wissenschaftliche Forschung, Forschung und Entwicklung
synonyms: die Untersuchung, die Studie, die Wissenschaft
image_context: A researcher working in a laboratory with scientific equipment
example_1: Die Universität investiert viel Geld in die Forschung.
example_1_translation: A universidade investe muito dinheiro em pesquisa.
example_2: Er betreibt Forschung auf dem Gebiet der Medizin.
example_2_translation: Ele realiza pesquisa na área da medicina.
example_3: Ihre Forschungen haben wichtige Ergebnisse geliefert.
example_3_translation: Suas pesquisas trouxeram resultados importantes.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: FOR-schung; 'sch' = /ʃ/
memory_hook: 'Forschen' = to research; think of 'force' + '-ing' — forcing your way to knowledge
related_words: forschen, der Forscher, die Wissenschaft, untersuchen
common_mistakes: Using plural 'Forschungen' when referring to the general concept (use singular 'die Forschung')

usage_notes: Can refer to merit-based or need-based financial support; 'ein Stipendium bekommen/erhalten' = to receive a scholarship
common_collocations: ein Stipendium erhalten, sich um ein Stipendium bewerben, das Stipendium verlieren
synonyms: das Förderstipendium, die Förderung, das Begabtenstipendium
image_context: A student receiving a scholarship certificate at an award ceremony
example_1: Sie hat ein Stipendium für ihr Studium in Wien bekommen.
example_1_translation: Ela recebeu uma bolsa de estudos para estudar em Viena.
example_2: Ohne das Stipendium könnte er sich das Studium nicht leisten.
example_2_translation: Sem a bolsa, ele não poderia pagar pelos estudos.
example_3: Er bewirbt sich um ein Stipendium der Humboldt-Stiftung.
example_3_translation: Ele está se candidatando a uma bolsa da Fundação Humboldt.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: sti-PEN-di-um; 'St' at start = /ʃt/
memory_hook: Very similar to English 'stipend' — financial support for studying
related_words: die Förderung, das DAAD-Stipendium, fördern, unterstützen
common_mistakes: Wrong plural: 'Stipendiums' instead of correct 'Stipendien'

usage_notes: In educational context 'Abschlussfeier' refers specifically to the graduation ceremony
common_collocations: die Abschlussfeier, den Abschluss feiern, die Abschlussarbeit
image_context: Students in caps and gowns celebrating at a graduation ceremony
example_1: Die Abschlussfeier findet im Juli statt.
example_1_translation: A cerimônia de formatura acontece em julho.
example_2: Nach der Graduierung beginnt sie sofort zu arbeiten.
example_2_translation: Após a formatura, ela começa a trabalhar imediatamente.
example_3: Alle Familien sind zur Abschlussfeier eingeladen.
example_3_translation: Todas as famílias estão convidadas para a cerimônia de formatura.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: AB-schluss-fei-er
memory_hook: Abschluss = the closing chapter of your studies
related_words: graduieren, die Zeremonie, das Diplom, abschließen
common_mistakes: Confusing 'Abschluss' (degree/graduation) with 'Schluss machen' (to break up)

usage_notes: Specifically a university lecture (professor speaks to large group); different from 'Unterricht' (school lessons) or 'Seminar' (smaller discussion group)
common_collocations: eine Vorlesung halten, die Vorlesung besuchen, in die Vorlesung gehen
image_context: A professor giving a lecture in a large university auditorium
example_1: Die Vorlesung beginnt um neun Uhr morgens.
example_1_translation: A aula começa às nove horas da manhã.
example_2: Er hat heute keine Vorlesung und bleibt zu Hause.
example_2_translation: Ele não tem aula hoje e fica em casa.
example_3: Die Professorin hält eine interessante Vorlesung über Geschichte.
example_3_translation: A professora dá uma aula interessante sobre história.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: VOR-le-sung; 'v' = /f/ in German
memory_hook: 'Vor' = before/in front, 'lesen' = to read — a lecture is reading aloud in front of people
related_words: der Hörsaal, der Professor, das Seminar, die Übung
common_mistakes: Using 'Vorlesung' for high school lessons — use 'Unterricht' instead

usage_notes: German universities divide the year into 'Wintersemester' (Oct–Feb) and 'Sommersemester' (Apr–Jul)
common_collocations: das erste Semester, im Sommersemester, das Wintersemester
image_context: A university academic calendar showing semester dates and deadlines
example_1: Im ersten Semester muss man viele Grundkurse belegen.
example_1_translation: No primeiro semestre, é preciso fazer muitas disciplinas básicas.
example_2: Das Wintersemester beginnt im Oktober.
example_2_translation: O semestre de inverno começa em outubro.
example_3: Sie ist jetzt im dritten Semester ihres Studiums.
example_3_translation: Ela está agora no terceiro semestre da sua graduação.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: se-MES-ter; 's' at start = /z/
memory_hook: Almost identical to English 'semester' — same Latin root
related_words: das Wintersemester, das Sommersemester, das Studienjahr, belegen
common_mistakes: Forgetting that plural 'Semester' is the same as singular

usage_notes: Refers specifically to a university-level teacher who is not yet a full professor; feminine form: 'die Dozentin'
common_collocations: der Dozent hält, ein Dozent an der Uni, der Gastdozent
image_context: A lecturer standing at the front of a university classroom
example_1: Der Dozent erklärt das Thema sehr verständlich.
example_1_translation: O professor explica o tema de forma muito compreensível.
example_2: Sie ist Dozentin für Linguistik an unserer Universität.
example_2_translation: Ela é professora de linguística na nossa universidade.
example_3: Der Gastdozent kommt aus den USA und lehrt hier für ein Jahr.
example_3_translation: O professor convidado vem dos EUA e leciona aqui por um ano.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: do-ZENT; 'z' = /ts/
memory_hook: Like 'docent' in English — someone who teaches or guides
related_words: der Professor, die Dozentin, lehren, unterrichten
common_mistakes: Confusing 'Dozent' (lecturer) with 'Professor' (full professor with chair)

usage_notes: Refers to the overall period or program of university study; 'das Studium aufnehmen' = to begin studies
common_collocations: das Studium beginnen, das Studium abbrechen, ein erfolgreiches Studium
image_context: A student surrounded by books studying at a university library
example_1: Sie hat ihr Studium mit Auszeichnung abgeschlossen.
example_1_translation: Ela concluiu seus estudos com distinção.
example_2: Das Medizinstudium dauert in Deutschland mindestens sechs Jahre.
example_2_translation: O curso de medicina na Alemanha dura pelo menos seis anos.
example_3: Er hat sein Studium nach zwei Jahren abgebrochen.
example_3_translation: Ele abandonou seus estudos após dois anos.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: STU-di-um; 'St' = /ʃt/
memory_hook: Related to English 'study' and 'studio' — a place/time for focused learning
related_words: studieren, der Student, die Universität, das Fach
common_mistakes: Using 'Studium' for school-level learning — use 'Schule' or 'Unterricht' instead

usage_notes: Context-dependent: in university = written assignment/term paper; at home = housework; for school = homework
common_collocations: eine Hausarbeit schreiben, die Hausarbeit abgeben, die Hausarbeit korrigieren
image_context: A student writing a term paper on a laptop with notes around
example_1: Ich muss bis Freitag eine Hausarbeit über Kant abgeben.
example_1_translation: Preciso entregar um trabalho sobre Kant até sexta-feira.
example_2: Seine Hausarbeit war sehr gut strukturiert.
example_2_translation: O trabalho dele estava muito bem estruturado.
example_3: Die Dozentin hat unsere Hausarbeiten mit Kommentaren zurückgegeben.
example_3_translation: A professora devolveu nossos trabalhos com comentários.
pronunciation_tips: Compound word: HAUS + ARBEIT; stress on first part: HAUS-ar-beit
memory_hook: 'Haus' = house/home, 'Arbeit' = work — work you take home
related_words: die Seminararbeit, die Abschlussarbeit, abgeben, schreiben
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'die Hausaufgabe' (school homework) in formal academic contexts

usage_notes: Reflexive: 'sich immatrikulieren'; used specifically for formal university enrollment; the document is 'die Immatrikulationsbescheinigung'
common_collocations: sich immatrikulieren, an einer Uni immatrikuliert sein, sich einschreiben
image_context: A student completing enrollment paperwork at a university office
example_1: Er hat sich im Oktober an der Universität Köln immatrikuliert.
example_1_translation: Ele se matriculou na Universidade de Colônia em outubro.
example_2: Um sich zu immatrikulieren, braucht man das Abitur.
example_2_translation: Para se matricular, é preciso ter o Abitur.
example_3: Sie ist seit zwei Semestern an unserer Uni immatrikuliert.
example_3_translation: Ela está matriculada na nossa universidade há dois semestres.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on fourth syllable: im-ma-tri-ku-LIE-ren; long 'ie' = /iː/
memory_hook: Related to 'matriculation' in English — the same formal registration process
related_words: die Immatrikulation, exmatrikulieren, einschreiben, anmelden
common_mistakes: Forgetting the reflexive 'sich': 'ich immatrikuliere mich' not 'ich immatrikuliere'

usage_notes: Refers to the specific academic program (e.g. Bachelor of Computer Science); compound of 'Studien' + 'Gang' (path/course)
common_collocations: einen Studiengang wählen, der Bachelorstudiengang, ein neuer Studiengang
image_context: A university catalog showing different degree program options
example_1: Welchen Studiengang hast du gewählt?
example_1_translation: Qual curso você escolheu?
example_2: Der Studiengang Informatik ist sehr beliebt.
example_2_translation: O curso de ciência da computação é muito popular.
example_3: Sie wechselt nach dem ersten Semester den Studiengang.
example_3_translation: Ela muda de curso após o primeiro semestre.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: STUDIEN-gang; stress on first part; 'St' = /ʃt/; 'g' at end = /ŋ/
memory_hook: 'Gang' means path or corridor — your educational path/corridor
related_words: das Fach, das Hauptfach, das Nebenfach, wechseln
common_mistakes: Using 'Studium' when the specific program is meant — 'Studiengang' is more precise

usage_notes: German grading: 1 (sehr gut) = best, 6 (ungenügend) = worst; opposite of English/Brazilian system
common_collocations: eine gute Note bekommen, die Note verbessern, mit einer Eins benotet werden
synonyms: die Zensur, die Benotung, die Bewertung
image_context: A German school report card showing grades from 1 to 6
example_1: Er hat in der Prüfung eine sehr gute Note bekommen.
example_1_translation: Ele tirou uma nota muito boa na prova.
example_2: In Deutschland ist die Note 1 die beste.
example_2_translation: Na Alemanha, a nota 1 é a melhor.
example_3: Sie möchte ihre Noten im nächsten Semester verbessern.
example_3_translation: Ela quer melhorar suas notas no próximo semestre.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: NO-te; 'o' is long: /oː/
memory_hook: Like musical 'note' but here it's the 'note' your teacher gives you for performance
related_words: benoten, das Zeugnis, der Notendurchschnitt, bestehen
common_mistakes: Confusing German scale (1=best) with Brazilian scale (10=best)

usage_notes: University library: 'die Universitätsbibliothek (UB)'; public library: 'die Stadtbibliothek'; to borrow: 'ausleihen'
common_collocations: in die Bibliothek gehen, ein Buch ausleihen, in der Bibliothek lernen
image_context: Students studying in a quiet university library surrounded by bookshelves
example_1: Ich gehe jeden Tag in die Bibliothek, um zu lernen.
example_1_translation: Eu vou à biblioteca todos os dias para estudar.
example_2: Du kannst das Buch kostenlos in der Bibliothek ausleihen.
example_2_translation: Você pode pegar o livro emprestado na biblioteca gratuitamente.
example_3: Die Universitätsbibliothek hat über eine Million Bücher.
example_3_translation: A biblioteca universitária tem mais de um milhão de livros.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: bib-li-O-thek; 'th' = /t/ (not like English 'th')
memory_hook: Same root as 'bibliography' — biblio (book) + thek (place) = place of books
related_words: das Buch, ausleihen, die Bücherei, der Lesesaal
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'th' as in English — in German it's a simple /t/

usage_notes: Refers to university admission ('Hochschulzulassung'); also used for product approval or vehicle registration; 'Numerus Clausus (NC)' determines university admission
common_collocations: eine Zulassung bekommen, die Zulassung beantragen, ohne Zulassung
image_context: A student reading an acceptance letter from a university
example_1: Sie hat eine Zulassung für den Medizinstudiengang erhalten.
example_1_translation: Ela recebeu uma admissão para o curso de medicina.
example_2: Die Zulassung hängt vom Numerus Clausus ab.
example_2_translation: A admissão depende da nota de corte.
example_3: Er wartet noch auf seine Zulassung von der Universität.
example_3_translation: Ele ainda está esperando sua admissão da universidade.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: ZU-las-sung; 'z' = /ts/
memory_hook: 'Zu' = to/towards, 'lassen' = to let — being let in (to university)
related_words: zulassen, die Bewerbung, der NC, ablehnen
common_mistakes: Confusing 'Zulassung' (admission) with 'Anmeldung' (registration/sign-up)

usage_notes: Specifically a large room for university lectures; smaller rooms are 'Seminarräume'; 'Hörsaal 1' is often abbreviated as 'HS 1'
common_collocations: im Hörsaal sitzen, den Hörsaal betreten, ein voller Hörsaal
image_context: A large tiered university lecture hall filled with students
example_1: Der Hörsaal fasst über dreihundert Studenten.
example_1_translation: O auditório comporta mais de trezentos alunos.
example_2: Wir treffen uns nach der Vorlesung vor dem Hörsaal.
example_2_translation: Nos encontramos depois da aula na frente do auditório.
example_3: Die Physikvorlesung findet im großen Hörsaal statt.
example_3_translation: A aula de física acontece no grande auditório.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: HÖR-saal; 'ö' = /øː/ (rounded front vowel); stress on first syllable
memory_hook: 'Hören' = to hear, 'Saal' = hall — a hall for hearing (lectures)
related_words: die Vorlesung, der Seminarraum, der Saal, hören
common_mistakes: Confusing plural 'Hörsäle' (umlaut change) with 'Hörsaalen'

usage_notes: Commonly used in academic writing; for degree thesis also 'die Abschlussarbeit' or 'die Dissertation' (doctoral); 'eine These aufstellen' = to put forward a thesis
common_collocations: eine These aufstellen, die These belegen, die Abschlussthesis
synonyms: die Abschlussarbeit, die Dissertation, die Behauptung
image_context: A graduate student writing their thesis at a desk with academic books
example_1: Ihre Thesis beschäftigt sich mit dem Klimawandel.
example_1_translation: A tese dela trata das mudanças climáticas.
example_2: Er braucht noch drei Monate, um seine Thesis fertigzustellen.
example_2_translation: Ele precisa de mais três meses para concluir a tese.
example_3: Die Professorin hat seine These sehr gut bewertet.
example_3_translation: A professora avaliou muito bem a tese dele.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: THE-sis; 'Th' = /t/ in German
memory_hook: Almost identical to English 'thesis' — academic argument or paper
related_words: die Dissertation, die Abschlussarbeit, die Bachelorarbeit, belegen
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'Th' as in English — in German it's simply /t/

usage_notes: Can mean academic subject ('Schulfach') or physical compartment ('Briefkastenfach'); context clarifies meaning
common_collocations: ein Fach studieren, das Lieblingsfach, das Hauptfach
synonyms: das Schulfach, das Fachgebiet, die Disziplin
image_context: A classroom shelf with different subject books and materials
example_1: Mein Lieblingsfach in der Schule war Biologie.
example_1_translation: Minha matéria favorita na escola era biologia.
example_2: Sie hat Mathematik als Hauptfach gewählt.
example_2_translation: Ela escolheu matemática como matéria principal.
example_3: Er ist Experte auf seinem Fachgebiet.
example_3_translation: Ele é especialista em sua área.
pronunciation_tips: Short vowel: /fax/; 'ch' after 'a' = /x/ (like Scottish 'loch')
memory_hook: Think of a 'fach' as a box or slot in a filing cabinet — each subject fits in its own slot
related_words: das Schulfach, das Hauptfach, das Nebenfach, fachlich
common_mistakes: Using 'Fach' for 'subject' in a book — use 'Thema' instead

primary_meaning: grade point average required for university admission (NC)
secondary_meaning: restricted access to a degree program
usage_notes: Often abbreviated 'NC'; uniquely German system where Abitur average determines access to competitive programs like Medicine or Law
common_collocations: der NC für Medizin, einen hohen NC haben, ohne NC studieren
image_search_query: university admission grade cutoff Germany NC
image_context: A chart showing minimum grade requirements for different German university programs
example_1: Für Medizin braucht man einen NC von mindestens 1,0.
example_1_translation: Para medicina, é necessária uma nota mínima de 1,0.
example_2: Der NC variiert je nach Universität und Bundesland.
example_2_translation: A nota de corte varia conforme a universidade e o estado.
example_3: Sie hat ihren NC verbessert, um Jura studieren zu können.
example_3_translation: Ela melhorou sua média para poder estudar direito.
pronunciation_tips: Latin phrase: NU-me-rus KLAU-sus; both words stressed on first syllable
memory_hook: 'Numerus' = number, 'Clausus' = closed — a closed number/limit for entry
related_words: die Zulassung, das Abitur, der Notendurchschnitt, die Hochschulreife
common_mistakes: Thinking NC is a score to achieve rather than a cutoff that changes each year

primary_meaning: to enroll in (a course), to register for
secondary_meaning: to prove, to demonstrate; to cover (a surface)
usage_notes: In academic context means to register for a course; also means to prove something with evidence ('mit Quellen belegen'); reflexive not used in this sense
common_collocations: einen Kurs belegen, ein Seminar belegen, einen Platz belegen
image_context: A student selecting and enrolling in courses on a university website
example_1: Im nächsten Semester möchte ich zwei Sprachkurse belegen.
example_1_translation: No próximo semestre, quero me inscrever em dois cursos de idiomas.
example_2: Hast du schon alle Kurse für dieses Semester belegt?
example_2_translation: Você já se inscreveu em todos os cursos deste semestre?
example_3: Er hat seine These mit vielen Quellen belegt.
example_3_translation: Ele fundamentou sua tese com muitas fontes.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: be-LE-gen; final 'g' softens to /n/ in infinitive
memory_hook: Think of 'belegen' as 'placing yourself' onto a course list
related_words: anmelden, einschreiben, der Kurs, das Seminar, abmelden
common_mistakes: Forgetting that 'belegen' has multiple meanings — context determines which applies

usage_notes: Required for library access, discounts, and campus facilities; also called 'Studierendenausweis' in gender-neutral language
common_collocations: den Studentenausweis vorzeigen, der Studentenausweis läuft ab, mit dem Studentenausweis
image_context: A German university student ID card with photo and student number
example_1: Zeig deinen Studentenausweis vor, um den Rabatt zu bekommen.
example_1_translation: Mostre sua carteirinha de estudante para obter o desconto.
example_2: Ich habe meinen Studentenausweis vergessen.
example_2_translation: Esqueci minha carteirinha de estudante.
example_3: Der Studentenausweis gilt auch als Semesterticket für den Bus.
example_3_translation: A carteirinha de estudante também vale como passe semestral de ônibus.
pronunciation_tips: Long compound: STU-den-ten-AUS-weis; stress on first and fourth syllables
memory_hook: 'Studenten' + 'Ausweis' (ID) — the identification for students
related_words: der Ausweis, die Immatrikulation, das Semesterticket, der Bibliotheksausweis
common_mistakes: Forgetting to renew each semester — it expires and must be validated

usage_notes: In academic context: a student presentation (oral or written); different from a full thesis; 'ein Referat halten' = to give a presentation
common_collocations: ein Referat halten, das Referat vorbereiten, ein Referat über etwas
synonyms: die Präsentation, der Vortrag, das Kurzreferat
image_context: A student giving an oral presentation in front of classmates in a seminar
example_1: Nächste Woche muss ich ein Referat über die Weimarer Republik halten.
example_1_translation: Na próxima semana, preciso fazer uma apresentação sobre a República de Weimar.
example_2: Sie hat ihr Referat sehr gut vorbereitet.
example_2_translation: Ela preparou muito bem sua apresentação.
example_3: Das Referat dauert etwa zwanzig Minuten.
example_3_translation: A apresentação dura cerca de vinte minutos.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: re-fe-RAT; rolled 'r' at start
memory_hook: Related to 'refer' — you present and refer to research you've done
related_words: halten, die Präsentation, der Vortrag, vortragen
common_mistakes: Confusing 'das Referat' (presentation) with 'das Büro' or 'die Abteilung' (department) in non-academic contexts

usage_notes: Used for school/university schedules; 'Stundenplan' is the weekly fixed timetable; different from 'Kalender' (general calendar)
common_collocations: der Stundenplan für die Woche, den Stundenplan erstellen, nach Stundenplan
image_context: A weekly class timetable pinned to a school notice board
example_1: Mein Stundenplan ist dieses Semester sehr voll.
example_1_translation: Minha grade horária está muito cheia neste semestre.
example_2: Hast du schon deinen neuen Stundenplan bekommen?
example_2_translation: Você já recebeu sua nova grade horária?
example_3: Der Stundenplan hängt am schwarzen Brett im Eingang.
example_3_translation: A grade horária está pendurada no quadro de avisos na entrada.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: STUN-den-plan; 'St' = /ʃt/; stress on first syllable
memory_hook: 'Stunden' = hours, 'Plan' = plan — a plan of your hours
related_words: die Stunde, der Plan, der Unterricht, der Lehrplan
common_mistakes: Using 'Stundenplan' for a personal to-do schedule — use 'Zeitplan' or 'Kalender' instead

usage_notes: European Union student mobility program; widely known as simply 'Erasmus'; 'Erasmus machen' = to do an Erasmus exchange semester
common_collocations: Erasmus machen, sich für Erasmus bewerben, das Erasmus-Stipendium
image_context: A group of international students from the Erasmus program together in Europe
example_1: Sie macht im dritten Semester Erasmus in Spanien.
example_1_translation: Ela faz Erasmus no terceiro semestre na Espanha.
example_2: Das Erasmus-Programm fördert den europäischen Austausch.
example_2_translation: O programa Erasmus incentiva o intercâmbio europeu.
example_3: Er hat sich für ein Erasmus-Stipendium in Italien beworben.
example_3_translation: Ele se candidatou a uma bolsa Erasmus na Itália.
pronunciation_tips: Stress: E-ras-mus; named after scholar Erasmus of Rotterdam
memory_hook: Named after Erasmus, the famous scholar who traveled across Europe — like modern exchange students
related_words: das Auslandsstudium, die Mobilität, das Stipendium, der Austausch
common_mistakes: Thinking it only covers studying — Erasmus also supports internships and volunteering
